# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Pipeline
on: 
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:  
  setup_build_env:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # specify the OS version that job will run on. (optional) if not specified, it defaults to latest Ubuntu LTS release available in GitHub Actions runner images for each workflow execution and pull request event triggering jobs only when explicitly stated here or as a parameter of an existing worklow
    steps: 
      - name: Checkout code   # Action that checks out your repository so your actions run on the right environment. (optional) if not specified, it defaults to using `$GITHUB_SHA` for commit and pull request events but can be configured otherwise in workflows using GitHub Actions' secrets feature
        uses: actions/checkout@v2  # specify a version of action that is used here. (optional) if not specified, the latest recommended action will always be used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
      - name: Setup NodeJS   # Action to setup nodejs environment for build process 
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2    # specify a version of action that is used here. (optional) if not specified, the latest recommended action will always be used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
        with: 
          node-version: '14'   # specify a version of Node.js that is installed here (optional) if not specified, the latest LTS will be used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example 
      - run: npm ci --silent   # Run a single command using the terminal/bash environment (optional) if not specified, defaults to running 'npm install' which is used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
      - run: npm test   # Run a single command using the terminal/bash environment (optional) if not specified, defaults to running 'npm install' which is used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
      - run: npm build   # Run a single command using the terminal/bash environment (optional) if not specified, defaults to running 'npm install' which is used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
      - run: npm start   # Run a single command using the terminal/bash environment (optional) if not specified, defaults to running 'npm install' which is used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
  deploy_production:    # Job that deploys production build. It will only run on the main branch push event (optional) if not specified, defaults to running 'npm start' which is used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
    needs: setup_build_env   # Specify a list of jobs that must run before the current one. (optional) if not specified, no dependencies are checked for running these specific actions when you use `needs` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest   # specify OS version that will run on the runner image. (optional) if not specified, defaults to using latest Ubuntu LTS release available for GitHub Actions Runner images by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
    environment: production   # Specify a set of env vars that will be passed into the deployment process. (optional) if not specified, no additional variables are used for deploying to Production by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
    strategy:   # Specify a set of strategies that will be applied during the deployment process. (optional) if not specified, defaults are used for deploying to Production by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
      run: npm start   # Run a single command using terminal/bash environment 
    steps:     # Specify the set of actions that will be executed during deployment process. (optional) if not specified, defaults to running 'npm test' which is used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
      - run: npm start   # Run a single command using the terminal/bash environment (optional) if not specified, defaults to running 'npm install' which is used by default when you use `actions` keyword with your step name or job name in workflows and steps below it like this as an example
